root_cause.text: The `readStored` method in `ZipArchiveInputStream` silently returns -1 when encountering a premature EOF (truncated entry) instead of throwing an exception, causing subsequent reads to return incorrect values and fail to consistently indicate the end of the stream.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces the silent `return -1;` with `buf.limit(0); throw new IOException("Truncated ZIP file");` when `in.read()` returns -1 inside `readStored()`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `readStored` method in `ZipArchiveInputStream` silently returns -1 when encountering a premature EOF (truncated entry) instead of throwing an exception, causing subsequent reads to return incorrect values and fail to consistently indicate the end of the stream.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces the silent `return -1;` with `buf.limit(0); throw new IOException("Truncated ZIP file");` when `in.read()` returns -1 inside `readStored()`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update changelog to document the bug fix for COMPRESS-463.
intent_record.what: Added a new action entry to the changelog XML file.
intent_record.how: Documents the fix for issue COMPRESS-463 regarding ZipArchiveInputStream#read behavior on corrupted entries.

intent_record.intent_summary: Throw an IOException instead of returning -1 when reading a truncated ZIP file.
intent_record.what: Replaced the return value of -1 with setting the buffer limit to 0 and throwing an IOException when reaching unexpected end of stream.
intent_record.how: Modifies the behavior on unexpected EOF to throw an error instead of silently returning -1, preventing issues caused by truncated ZIP files.

intent_record.intent_summary: Enhance test suite to verify consistent exception behavior on truncated zip streams.
intent_record.what: Adds a test block to verify that attempting to read from a zip input stream after an exception continues to throw the same exception.
intent_record.how: Validates the consistent failure state of a truncated entry by expecting an IOException on subsequent reads.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add tests to verify exception handling for corrupted stored ZIP entries.
intent_record.what: Added two new test methods to verify behavior when reading corrupted stored ZIP entries.
intent_record.how: Ensures that both single-byte and multi-byte reads throw an IOException with the message 'Truncated ZIP file' when encountering a corrupted entry.

grouping_result.intent: Update changelog to document the bug fix for COMPRESS-463.
Throw an IOException instead of returning -1 when reading a truncated ZIP file.
Add tests to verify exception handling for corrupted stored ZIP entries.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add tests to verify exception handling for corrupted or truncated ZIP entries and streams.

relevance_review.reason: The group only updates the changelog to document the bug fix and does not contain code changes that address the vulnerability root cause.
The group's core intent directly addresses the vulnerability by throwing an IOException instead of returning -1 when reading a truncated ZIP file, which matches the described root cause.
The group adds tests that specifically verify the correct exception handling for truncated and corrupted ZIP entries, which directly corresponds to the vulnerability's root cause.

candidates.rationale: This statement silently returns -1 upon encountering a premature EOF instead of throwing an exception, which allows subsequent reads to return incorrect values and fail to indicate the end of the stream as described in the root cause.
This condition triggers the erroneous return of -1 on unexpected EOF.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement silently returns -1 upon encountering a premature EOF instead of throwing an exception, which allows subsequent reads to return incorrect values.
This condition checks for EOF and triggers the erroneous silent return of -1 instead of handling the truncated entry correctly.

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/zip/ZipArchiveInputStream.java b/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/zip/ZipAr
bic_trace_step.reason: The `readStored` method contains the vulnerable logic `if ((buf.lengthOfLastRead = in.read(buf.buf)) == -1) { return -1; }`, which silently returns -1 on premature EOF instead of throwing an exception. The commit only applies whitespace and formatting changes, leaving the bug intact.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: separate state of stream from state of entry current being read from state of temporary buffer data is read into.  Docum

commit 04e132b74b62cac7bb2d171825d5975e4fbfc155
Author: Stefan Bodewig <bodewig@apache.org>

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/zip/ZipArchiveInputStream.java b/
bic_trace_step.reason: In the `read` method, when reading STORED entries without a data descriptor, the code checks `if ((buf.lengthOfLastRead = in.read(buf.buf)) == -1)` and silently returns `-1` even if `current.bytesRead` has not yet reached the expected `csize`. This matches the root cause where a truncated entry causes premature EOF without throwing an exception.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 008ca948dadacea8d1a2662c577fdcb25f32d5e2
Author: Stefan Bodewig <bodewig@apache.org>

    support as much as possible of ZipFile as a stream can do in ZipArchiveInputStream, COMPRESS-47
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/commons/proper/compress/trunk@758896 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/zip/ZipArchiveInputStream.java b/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress
bic_trace_step.reason: The `read` method handles STORED entries inline and contains the vulnerable logic. Specifically, if the underlying stream reaches EOF prematurely (`(lengthOfLastRead = in.read(buf)) == -1`), it silently returns -1 instead of throwing an `EOFException`, which causes truncated entries to appear as completely read.
